question_text: Explain why the Northern and Southern Hemispheres experience opposite seasons at the same time of year.
correct_answer: Earth's axis is tilted at 23.5°. When the Northern Hemisphere is tilted towards the Sun (summer), the Southern Hemisphere is tilted away (winter). As Earth orbits, this tilt causes opposite seasons in each hemisphere.
explanation: The tilt causes one hemisphere to receive more direct sunlight (and longer days) while the other receives less direct sunlight (and shorter days). This creates opposite seasons.
